channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on wall -peckham high st, 11th May
item.description:
Please cover these tags

Report on FixMyStreet

Fly tipped broke / old coffee machine. left on the wall.

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: 193 new Kent road, 11th May
item.description:
Rubbish. You said you’d removed it on Sunday morning but it’s still here? Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): New Kent Road, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass and debris left all over local roads, 11th May
item.description:
Hi, the rubbish teams arrived this morning to collect recycling (blue bins) and for some reason they have left the streets littered in bin debris including smashed glass all over Bramshott, eversley and Sandtoft.. Possibly others too but these are the roads we’ve been down today. It’s unacceptable and dangerous to cars and pedestrians. Sharp tin cans broken glass and so much litter that I’ve ever seen. Absolutely disgusting behaviour from the normally efficient team. Please send a clean up operation before someone is hurt or an animal is injured. Equally a car had to swerve to avoid a large piece of debris. I simply don’t understand how they made such a mess or why they found D it ok to just leave the streets littered this way.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramshot Avenue, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parking on yellow line, 11th May
item.description:
The car park on yellow line.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 58, High Road, Hampstead Garden Suburb, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown area, 11th May
item.description:
Repeatedly reported in past - nothing seems to be done. The overgrowth juts out 18" to 2 ft forcing pedestrians towards the road & risk. Debris collects underneath. Basal growth and/or shrubs also block view of pedestrians & drivers. SJS/Conway advise contractor advises not within coverage. Who is responsible? When can we expect action on safety grounds?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): London Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No Signage causing Noise, 11th May
item.description:
Hello, I hope you are well. There are road works happening on Hornsey Park Road / Mayers Road, that are due to continue for many months now. There is no signage anywhere on Hornsey Park Road warning of temporary traffic lights ahead. Because the traffic lights are on Mayes road, drivers can see them round the corner on HPR. This means that all day, every day, people are honking their horns in frustration nothing is moving. Can you please put up some signage so people know they are waiting for lights. It causes a lot of noise pollution, and is extremely disruptive for anyone like me working from home. Many thanks, Georgie
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hornsey Park Road, Hornsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 11th May
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 11/05/26 11:38 50 curtismill wy(adj alley 10 yrds in) brken bottle glass a x f/way With add'nal lit pik mcdonalds bags left next to glass
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Curtismill Way, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Redundant warning signs to vehicles left in street, 11th May
item.description:
Last week some workers built a crossover and others filled some potholes. One or the other placed temporary road signs either side of the work. They have long since moved on but they have left 2 now redundant warning signs behind. They need to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Drive, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road cracked between 27 and 28 Jenton Avenue., 11th May
item.description:
The crack was noted over 3 weeks and is getting worse with the daily passing of the cars, vans and lorries over this cracked road/street. Further down the street, a similar previous crack has been repaired but unsatisfactory: pictures sent.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jenton Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars are driving on pavement to cut through, 11th May
item.description:
We need a barrier put up to stop commercil vans and card driving through fro residental to industrial estate

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eleanor Cross Road, Waltham Cross, Broxbourne
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Public litter bin still not emptied, 11th May
item.description:
Over a week now this has been not emptied. always the same problem with this bin. I live directly opposite it and everytime I come out of my flat have to look at this mess.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blakeney Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree in Passage between Gondar Gardens and Sarre Road, 11th May
item.description:
Half dead tree and there is also another one in the passage about to touch our house wall - My property is Flat 1, 48 Sarre Road, London NW2 3SL

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gondar Gardens, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped either side of the pathway, 11th May
item.description:
Rubbish has started appearing along the pathway within Sidcup sports centre grounds. Can you please attend and remove? Maybe a litter bin nearby would help?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hurst Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped household waste outside 191 lessons hill, 11th May
item.description:
Flytipped household rubbish. Junk has been repeatedly dumped here by bad faith actors for over a week. Please can it be cleared.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 191, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
